sections.sourcing: No new sourcing-country shifts reported this week. Tesco's F&F supply base remains anchored across China, India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka and Turkey, coordinated by the ~300-person Tesco Sourcing team headquartered in Hong Kong, with Sri Lanka reaffirmed in recent trade coverage as a 'significant' long-term sourcing destination.
sections.strategy: No fresh strategy announcements surfaced this week. Ongoing context: F&F's May 2026 online relaunch (2,200+ products via Tesco.com and the Clubcard app) continues to drive non-food sales growth, with clothing and home MD Katja Ahola Klamkin (joined from H&M Group) overseeing further planned range expansion online 'over the coming months'.
sections.launches: No new product launch news this week beyond the already-live spring/summer 2026 range (co-ord sets, midi dresses, elevated basics, most under £40), which remains on shelf and online.
soWhat: Stable five-country sourcing footprint (China, India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Turkey) with no signalled shifts means current supplier allocations are safe for now — no urgent repositioning needed this week.

F&F's continued sales momentum (FY sales up 5.1% to £1.2bn, plus the digital relaunch) points to rising order volumes ahead; factories with headroom should be ready to field increased capacity requests as online range expansion continues.

New clothing/home MD arriving from H&M Group raises the chance that H&M-style sourcing discipline, sustainability requirements (e.g. tighter Better Cotton/recycled content targets), or vendor consolidation could be introduced in coming quarters — worth monitoring for early signals.
